nativeRange.habitat: Lagoon and seaward reefs; protected coastal waters at depths of 1–16 m. Exclusively associated with the bubble-tip anemone Entacmaea quadricolor.

diet.notes: Generally accepts most meaty foods (mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, frozen carnivore preparations) as well as herbivore preparations and marine flake food. Feeding 2–3 times daily is commonly advised. In the wild, tends to eat zooplankton and benthic algae.

imageCandidates.notes: Photographer: Bernard DUPONT (from France). Taken 2009-10-05 at Bunaken Island, Sulawesi, Indonesia. Subject explicitly identified as Premnas biaculeatus in filename and Commons page. Full-resolution file; high confidence of correct subject. This is the wild-type white-stripe form — the lighting morph is a captive-bred variant, so no morph-specific Wikimedia image exists.

Photographer: Petrinos, C.; locality Indonesia, North Sulawesi; life stage juvenile. FishBase page for Premnas biaculeatus (species ID 6632). All rights reserved per FishBase copyright notice; use only as fallback.

Product hero image on LiveAquaria species page for the Lightning Maroon Clownfish captive-bred morph. This is the only readily available image of the actual lightning stripe morph (with broken, jagged white patterning). Exact image URL not extractable from page fetch; this is the product page URL. Recommended as fallback if a morph-specific image is required.

summary: A captive-bred designer variant of the Maroon Clownfish, distinguished by broken, lightning-bolt-like white bar patterning unique to each individual. Generally easy to keep with stable water conditions and a bubble-tip anemone.
careNotes:
The Lightning Maroon Clownfish is a selectively bred color morph of Premnas biaculeatus, the same species as the Gold Stripe Maroon Clownfish. Its distinctive broken, jagged white bars — resembling lightning bolt patterns — are unique to each individual and tend to become more pronounced as the fish matures. The morph originated from a single wild-caught Papua New Guinea specimen and is now captive-bred widely.

Like its parent species, this is generally considered easy to keep once established. Two caveats are worth noting: (1) the species tends to be notably aggressive toward other clownfish and conspecifics, so it is best kept singly or as a mated pair — one specimen per tank is strongly advised unless a bonded pair is available; (2) maroon clownfish have been reported to be somewhat more susceptible to common saltwater diseases (ich, brooklynella) than other clownfish species, so proper quarantine and stable water parameters are particularly important.

Temperature: sources disagree — LiveAquaria gives 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C), AquaticCommunity gives 75–82°F (23.9–27.8°C), and FishBase wild data gives 26.7–29.3°C. Averaged across the three sources to min 24.0°C / max 27.5°C.

This species is sensitive to copper-based medications; exposure should be kept below 0.15 ppm. When netting, avoid mesh nets as the cheek spines can become entangled — use a specimen container instead.

Exclusively associates with Entacmaea quadricolor (bubble-tip anemone) in the wild; will generally accept other anemones in captivity but E. quadricolor is strongly preferred. Sequential hermaphrodites — the dominant (largest) individual becomes female. Captive-bred specimens are recommended over wild-caught as they tend to be hardier and more disease-resistant.
breedingNotes: Has been reported to breed readily in home aquariums once a bonded pair is established. Eggs are demersal, adhesive, and laid near the host anemone; males guard and aerate the eggs until hatching, typically in 6–11 days. Fry require rotifers initially, then Artemia nauplii. Acquiring a mated pair or introducing a juvenile male alongside a young female is the commonly advised approach. Breeding the lightning morph produces offspring with a range of patterning; the lightning trait can appear in offspring but is not guaranteed in all individuals.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Direct species product page for Captive-Bred Lightning Maroon Clownfish. Provided adult size (6 in), temperature (72–78°F), pH (8.1–8.4), salinity (1.020–1.025), min tank (30 gal), care level (Easy), diet (omnivore), and reef safe status.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-verification for taxonomy and wild parameters. Max size 17.0 cm; wild temperature range 26.7–29.3°C. Taxonomic family Pomacentridae confirmed. Note: FishBase lists the species under the synonym Amphiprion biaculeatus; Premnas biaculeatus is the currently accepted aquarium trade name. pH not provided by FishBase.

additional.name: AquaticCommunity
additional.notes: Secondary cross-verification. Confirmed adult size (15 cm / 6 in), pH (8.1–8.4), salinity (1.020–1.025), kH (8–12), min tank 30 gal / 120 L. Temperature given as 75–82°F (23.9–27.8°C) — slightly warmer than LiveAquaria. Care level listed as intermediate to advanced due to disease susceptibility (contrasted with LiveAquaria's 'Easy'). Per playbook rules, careLevel set to beginner (easy overall for captive-bred specimens) with disease susceptibility documented in careNotes.

additional.name: Wikipedia – Maroon clownfish
additional.notes: Used for native range cross-check and Lightning Maroon variant confirmation. Article notes the lightning trait as a selectively bred morph now common in the trade. Wikimedia Commons image identified here for imageCandidates.

fish.breedingNotes: Eggs demersal, laid near host anemone; male guards and aerates. Fry require rotifers then Artemia. Pair bonding essential. Lightning patterning is heritable but not guaranteed in all offspring.
